title: 〜おかげで
description: 〜おかげで credits a good outcome to a cause — "thanks to." It is the positive counterpart of 〜せいで. Attach it to a noun with の or to a plain-form clause.

content.text: 〜おかげで expresses gratitude: something turned out well, and you're crediting the cause. Attach おかげで to a noun with の (先生（せんせい）のおかげで, "thanks to my teacher") or directly to a plain-form clause (薬（くすり）を飲（の）んだおかげで, "thanks to taking the medicine"). It comes from お陰（かげ）, literally "honorable shade/shelter."

content.japanese: noun ＋ の ＋ おかげで　／　plain form ＋ おかげで　（thanks to — good outcome）
みんなのおかげで成功（せいこう）しました。
content.english: Thanks to everyone, it was a success.

content.japanese: 先生（せんせい）のおかげで試験（しけん）に合格（ごうかく）できました。
content.english: Thanks to my teacher, I was able to pass the exam.

content.japanese: 薬（くすり）を飲（の）んだおかげで熱（ねつ）が下（さ）がりました。
content.english: Thanks to taking the medicine, my fever went down.

content.japanese: あなたが手伝（てつだ）ってくれたおかげで早（はや）く終（お）わりました。
content.english: Thanks to your help, I finished early.

content.text: おかげさまで and Ironic Use

おかげさまで is a set polite phrase — "thanks to you / thankfully" — used to answer after-you-well questions even when the listener did nothing specific. おかげで can also be turned sarcastic: pairing it with a bad result flips it into pointed blame, like saying "thanks a lot" through gritted teeth.

content.japanese: おかげさまで　（thankfully / thanks to you — set polite phrase）
おかげさまで、家族（かぞく）はみんな元気（げんき）です。
content.english: Thankfully, my whole family is doing well.

content.japanese: 君（きみ）のおかげで計画（けいかく）が台無（だいな）しだよ。
content.english: Thanks to you, the plan is ruined. (sarcastic)

content.text: おかげで vs せいで vs ため

All three link a result to a cause, but the attitude differs. 〜おかげで gives credit — the outcome is good and you're grateful (先生（せんせい）のおかげで合格（ごうかく）した). 〜せいで assigns blame — the outcome is bad and you're pointing a finger (渋滞（じゅうたい）のせいで遅刻（ちこく）した). 〜ため is neutral and formal — it just states a cause with no praise or blame and fits written announcements (台風（たいふう）のため中止（ちゅうし）). Choose おかげで for good news, せいで for bad, and ため when you want to stay impartial.

examples.japanese: noun ＋ の ＋ おかげで　／　plain form ＋ おかげで　（thanks to — good outcome）
みんなのおかげで成功（せいこう）しました。
examples.english: Thanks to everyone, it was a success.

examples.japanese: 先生（せんせい）のおかげで試験（しけん）に合格（ごうかく）できました。
examples.english: Thanks to my teacher, I was able to pass the exam.

examples.japanese: 薬（くすり）を飲（の）んだおかげで熱（ねつ）が下（さ）がりました。
examples.english: Thanks to taking the medicine, my fever went down.

examples.japanese: あなたが手伝（てつだ）ってくれたおかげで早（はや）く終（お）わりました。
examples.english: Thanks to your help, I finished early.

examples.japanese: おかげさまで　（thankfully / thanks to you — set polite phrase）
おかげさまで、家族（かぞく）はみんな元気（げんき）です。
examples.english: Thankfully, my whole family is doing well.

examples.japanese: 君（きみ）のおかげで計画（けいかく）が台無（だいな）しだよ。
examples.english: Thanks to you, the plan is ruined. (sarcastic)
